task_completion.description: Agent edits metadata fields to correct discrepancies.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the right-hand metadata pane in Juris-M, editing fields such as Reporter, Court, Date, Volume, or Pages.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the required collection hierarchy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left pane of Juris-M shows a parent folder named 'Fourth Amendment Digital Privacy' containing four subfolders: 'Foundational Precedent', 'Digital Privacy Doctrine', 'Scholarly Analysis', and 'Limiting Authority'.

task_completion.description: Agent assigns items to the appropriate subcollections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show items being dragged into the subcollections, or the middle pane displaying the correct filtered items when a subcollection is selected.

task_completion.description: Agent creates 'Related' links between specified case-article pairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Related' tab in the right-hand pane of Juris-M is open for an item, showing a linked item (e.g., Carpenter linked to Freiwald, or Katz linked to Kerr).

task_completion.description: Agent sets the Quick Copy citation style to 'JM Indigo (Law Review)'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M Preferences window is open to the 'Export' tab, with 'JM Indigo (Law Review)' selected in the Default Format dropdown.

task_completion.description: Agent exports the library as an RIS file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Export or Save dialog is visible, showing the format set to RIS and the file being saved as 'symposium_bibliography.ris' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Juris-M GUI to edit metadata and organize collections.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Juris-M application interface rather than terminal commands modifying the SQLite database directly.

integrity.description: Agent used the Juris-M export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Juris-M 'Export Library' dialog being used, rather than a script generating the RIS file.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Juris-M window is visible and actively being used, not just a text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Metadata corrections required: Reporters for Katz, Miller, Jardines should be 'U.S.'. Courts for Mapp, Smith, Kyllo should be 'United States Supreme Court'. Dates: Terry (1968-06-10), Riley (2014-06-25), Freiwald (2011). First page: Gates (213). Volumes: Solove (154), Ohm (57). Pages: Henderson (975-1036). Collections: Parent 'Fourth Amendment Digital Privacy'. Subcollections: 'Foundational Precedent' (Mapp, Katz, Terry, Jardines), 'Digital Privacy Doctrine' (Kyllo, Jones, Riley, Carpenter), 'Limiting Authority' (Miller, Smith, Gates), 'Scholarly Analysis' (all 7 articles). Related pairs: Carpenter-Freiwald, Katz-Kerr, Smith-Murphy, Riley-Tokson. Export path: /home/ga/Documents/symposium_bibliography.ris.
